title: Youtube video generation and automation
content:
Creating YouTube content is exhausting. Before a single viewer even sees your work, you have to:

🖋️ Write an engaging script
🎨 Generate or source visuals
🎙️ Record a high-quality voiceover
✂️ Edit the video and add transitions
⏱️ Painstakingly sync subtitles
🔍 Optimize the SEO (title, description, tags)
📤 Manually upload and schedule

I asked myself: What if I could automate this entire process from end to end—running completely locally on my machine, without spending a dime on API keys? 💡

The Project: AI YouTube Video Engine Over the past few weeks, I challenged myself to build a fully automated YouTube pipeline.
Here is exactly what the engine does from a single command:

The Input: You provide a single motivational quote.

The Story: It writes an emotionally engaging story.

The Visuals: It generates cinematic images specifically tailored to the story prompts.

The Audio: It synthesizes a natural voiceover and word-level synced subtitles.

The Edit: It automatically applies Ken Burns zoom effects and crossfade transitions.

The SEO: It generates optimized metadata, titles, and trending hashtags.

The Output: It natively uploads and schedules the final video to YouTube! 🚀

What I Learned & The Tech Stack: Building this pushed me to integrate a complex array of AI tools. Here’s a look under the hood at what I used and learned:

🧠 Local LLM Orchestration: Used Ollama (gemma3:4b) to dynamically generate unique stories and SEO metadata.

🖼️ GPU-Accelerated Images: Learned how to generate high-quality scenes locally using Stable Diffusion XL, managing VRAM effectively.

🗣️ Audio Processing: Integrated Kokoro TTS for realistic voice synthesis and OpenAI Whisper for precise subtitle timing.

🎞️ Programmatic Editing: Used MoviePy and FFmpeg to assemble the media, applying complex transitions through code.

🏗️ Clean Architecture: Designed a production-ready, 4-layer Python architecture (Pydantic config, custom exceptions, Loguru tracking).

📤 API Automation: Automated scheduling and publishing using the YouTube Data API v3.

This project was a massive leap from a static script to a high-quality automated pipeline.

📺 See the results: Check out the videos generated entirely by this engine: https://lnkd.in/gQzpvAbz

💻 Dive into the code: The entire project is open-source! Check it out and leave a ⭐ if you find it interesting: https://lnkd.in/gvhkVPKR

I’d love to hear your thoughts or answer any questions about the architecture or the models used. Let's connect! 👇

#AI #Python #MachineLearning #YouTubeAutomation #StableDiffusion hashtag#LLM #SoftwareEngineering #OpenSource #TechJourney #Developer
